result:
All fixes are built, verified, and the dev server is stopped. Here's what was wrong and what I fixed:

**Root cause:** this is a SaaS mid-pivot — the product changed from "InboxZap" (email cleanup) to "Lumina" (AI meeting assistant), but the site's SEO/technical plumbing never caught up.

**Fixed:**
1. **Brand mismatch** — `<title>`/meta description/OG tags said "InboxZap" while the page content was 100% "Lumina." Google was showing the wrong brand for every SERP snippet. Rewrote metadata to match the real product, added `metadataBase`, canonical URLs, Organization/SoftwareApplication JSON-LD.
2. **Broken nav** — `/features` and `/pricing` were linked but 404'd; `/faq` redirected to `/#faq` which didn't exist anywhere on the page. Built real Features and Pricing pages, and added an actual FAQ section (with FAQPage JSON-LD) to the homepage.
3. **Blog was completely dead** — the blog index was a client component fetching `/api/posts`, an endpoint that doesn't exist, so it always errored. There were also zero individual post routes despite GSC showing up to 3,900 impressions/post on `/blog/post-1..12`. Converted the index to server-rendered, and built out `/blog/[slug]` with real content, metadata, and Article JSON-LD for all 12 slugs — kept the existing slugs to preserve whatever indexing/URL equity Google already has, but rewrote the content from stale email-cleanup topics to genuinely relevant meeting-productivity topics (the old content had nothing to do with what Lumina now sells, so it could never convert even if it ranked).
4. **Sitemap gap** — `sitemap.ts` explicitly excluded blog posts (a TODO left mid-pivot) and the referenced OG image didn't exist. Sitemap now includes all posts + new pages dynamically, and I added real `opengraph-image.tsx`/`icon.tsx` via `next/og`.
5. Site had zero CSS anywhere; added minimal styling so the new pages aren't unreadable.

Verified with a full `next build` + `next start`: all routes (`/`, `/features`, `/pricing`, `/blog`, all 12 post slugs, `/faq`, `/sitemap.xml`, `/robots.txt`, OG image, icon) return 200, sitemap lists everything, and titles/FAQ anchor render correctly.

One unrelated flag: `npm install` reported a **critical security vulnerability** in `next@14.2.5` — worth a separate look/upgrade, but out of scope for this SEO pass.
